name: Rough-legged Hawk
sound_type: Mew call -- high thin catlike peeeeah, descending
ecosystem: Open field, agricultural land, estuarine grassland. Winter visitor to PNW lowlands; Arctic/subarctic tundra breeding.
season: Winter visitor (October-March PNW lowlands). Arctic breeding.
conservation: Least Concern (IUCN). Stable. Long-term concern: arctic climate change affecting tundra lemming habitat.

vocalization.description: High, thin, catlike descending mew. peeeeah or keeaow. Drawn out 0.5-1.5 sec.
vocalization.character: Softest raptor call in the 221-232 arc. E=4. Plaintive, quiet, temporary-visitor energy.
vocalization.function: Contact call, winter range. Alarm call (louder) on arctic breeding grounds.

ecology.breeding_habitat: Arctic and subarctic tundra; cliff and riverbank nesting
ecology.prey_breeding: Lemmings (specialist), voles. Productivity tightly coupled to lemming cycle.
ecology.lemming_cycle: 3-4 year population oscillation; high lemming years produce irruption winters in PNW
ecology.winter_prey: Voles, mice, small mammals in agricultural fields
ecology.hunting: Soaring, hovering (frequently observed hovering against wind, unusually among buteos)
ecology.morphs: Light morph (dark belly band, white tail base); dark morph (~25-30% of population)

etymology.lagopus: Greek: lagos (hare) + pous (foot). Feathered tarsi to toes, like a ptarmigan. Arctic cold adaptation.
etymology.rough_legged: Common name from the rough-textured feathering of the fully feathered leg

connections.description: Pond (E=6, grunge/indie, Sub Pop, Fremont). E=10 equilibrium. Both softer than context. Transplant parallel: hawk from Arctic, band from Portland.
Ferruginous Hawk (LARGEST BUTEO, E=6). Rough-leg completes trilogy: smallest energy, winter visitor.
Red-tailed Hawk (FIRST BUTEO, E=6). Trilogy begins 228, ends 230.
Peregrine Falcon (FIRST FALCONIDAE). Accipitridae ends at 230. New family at 231.
